steps.title: Separate the clauses
steps.content: The words before the blank form one independent clause: the shoulder contains a muscle attached to the scapula. The words after the blank form another independent clause: this muscle helps rotate the upper arm.

steps.title: Join complete clauses
steps.content: Choice A creates a comma splice, and Choice C creates a run-on. Choice D illogically makes the scapula the thing that plays a role in rotating the arm. A semicolon correctly joins the two related complete clauses. The answer is B.
